question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: External Teams calls with one‑way audio (internal OK, external silent) in Zurich were previously diagnosed as a media/firewall/NAT configuration issue affecting media ports or session timers. Resolution required Unified Communications/SBC and network adjustments according to the Direct Routing architecture (changes made by engineers; tests with SBC/session parameters; firewall/NAT timeouts adjusted). This is documented in tickets TI-1602241 and TI-1604879
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 5:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – No audio in external calls
Ticket number: TI-1602241
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 14
Owner: Employee 33
Origin: Phone
Type: Incident
Created on: 08.07.25 08:21
Closure
Closure date: 18.07.25 16:05
Closure text: Configuration adjusted according to Direct Routing architecture. Voice transmission stable again.
Activities
08.07.25 08:10
Call from Mr. Contact Person 14.
He reports that several employees since this morning cannot hear audio during external phone calls in Microsoft Teams. The call is established, the external participant can hear the employees, but they themselves hear nothing. It is described as “completely silent”.
Internal Teams calls function normally. Chats and video conferences are also not affected.
08.07.25 08:21
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33.
08.07.25 09:05 – Email from Employee 33 to Contact Person 14
Good morning Mr. Contact Person 14
Thank you for your report.
Could you please briefly confirm the following points:
– Does it affect only external calls (mobile/fixed line)?
– Are both incoming and outgoing calls affected?
– Does the issue occur for all users or only specific ones?
– Since when has the issue existed?
Thank you for your feedback.
Kind regards
08.07.25 09:34 – Response from Contact Person 14
Good morning
Yes, it affects only external calls.
Both incoming and outgoing.
At the moment, about a dozen people are affected, all at the Zurich location.
Since this morning, everything was normal yesterday.
Kind regards
08.07.25 10:12 – Internal note
Test call performed with user S. Meier.
Internal Teams call works.
External call to mobile phone: connection established, conversation partner confirms he can hear Mr. Meier, Mr. Meier hears nothing.
Teams shows status “Connected”. No error message.
08.07.25 11:40 – Email to customer
We have restarted the Teams client and cleared the cache as a test.
Could you please check with two additional affected users whether the behavior is identical?
08.07.25 13:05 – Customer response
We tested with three additional persons.
Same behavior everywhere.
Headsets differ (Jabra, Poly).
LAN connection.
Home office users do not seem to be affected.
09.07.25 08:50 – Customer contacts again
Good morning
We just remembered that a firewall adjustment (security hardening) was carried out over the weekend.
Could this have an impact?
Kind regards
09.07.25 09:30 – Internal note
Firewall change timing plausible.
Escalated to Unified Communications / Network team.
09.07.25 10:22 – Ticket taken over by System Engineer 18
09.07.25 11:15 – Email from System Engineer 18 to customer
Good day
We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing configuration.
Signaling (SIP) appears to function correctly, as calls can be established.
We are currently checking the media flow (audio stream).
We will get back to you as soon as we have further findings.
Kind regards
10.07.25 08:40 – Internal analysis
SBC shows successful SIP connections.
No call drops.
Media negotiation (SDP) is performed.
Notable:
RTP traffic appears stable only in one direction.
10.07.25 10:50 – Firewall rule comparison
New rule implemented at Zurich location.
Outbound traffic configured more restrictively.
Comparison with Microsoft documentation shows deviation in media port ranges.
10.07.25 14:15 – Internal test phase
Temporary adjustment of firewall configuration for media ports.
Test call performed.
Audio immediately available in both directions.
Firewall reverted to restrictive rule.
Issue occurs again.
11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer
Good day
We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location.
Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked.
We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification.
We will provide an update shortly.
Kind regards
14.07.25 – Internal documentation review
Comparison with internal document
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1”
Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled.
Finding:
Zurich location configured with Media Bypass.
Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range.
16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment
Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
17.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We tested several external calls.
Audio works normally again.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you for your support.
Kind regards
18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 14
The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment.
The configuration was corrected according to the internal Direct Routing architecture specification.
Voice transmission is now stable again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External calls disconnect after approx. 30 seconds
Ticket number: TI-1604879
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 16
Owner: Employee 35
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 22.07.25 09:14
Closure
Closure date: 31.07.25 15:52
Closure text: Analysis completed. Adjustment made according to Direct Routing architecture. Calls stable.
Activities
22.07.25 09:02 – Email from Contact Person 16 to Helpdesk
Good morning
Since yesterday, several employees report that external calls in Microsoft Teams disconnect after approximately 30 seconds.
The call is established normally, both parties can hear each other, but the connection is terminated after a short time.
Internal Teams calls are not affected.
Could you please review this?
Kind regards
22.07.25 09:14
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35.
22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Thank you for your report.
Could you please confirm:
– Does it affect incoming and outgoing calls?
– Does the disconnection always occur after approximately the same duration?
– Are all locations affected or only a specific one?
Thank you very much.
22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response
It only affects the Basel location.
Both incoming and outgoing calls.
The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier.
Home office users are not affected.
22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note
Test performed with affected user.
External call to mobile phone.
Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds.
No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.”
23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis
Possible client error or unstable connection.
Test with another device → same behavior.
Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change.
23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer
Good day
We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing logs.
Signaling is established correctly.
The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client.
Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location?
Kind regards
23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response
On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented.
A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created.
Could this be relevant?
24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis
SBC logs reviewed:
– SIP INVITE successful
– 200 OK correct
– Call established
– After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC
No obvious error code.
24.07.25 10:30 – Firewall analysis
No blocking visible.
Ports open according to standard configuration.
24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis
Session-Expires header reviewed.
Session timer set to 30 seconds.
Comparison with other locations shows:
There, 180 seconds.
25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation
Document:
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1”
Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant.
Note in the blueprint:
For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination.
25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests
Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC.
Test call performed.
Call remains stable for several minutes.
Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible.
28.07.25 – Coordination with network team
New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy.
UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds.
Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds.
29.07.25 – Adjustment
NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
30.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We conducted several test calls.
No more disconnections observed.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you very much.
31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 16
The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Calls now remain stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Test 3 – External landline call → No audio

Measures:

09.07.25 08:55

09.07.25 10:22

In-depth analysis
09.07.25 11:15
Analysis in Teams Admin Center:
• Direct Routing active
• SBC status: Online
• PSTN calls are signaled
• No errors in SIP signaling
Notable:

10.07.25 08:40

• SIP INVITE correct

RTP stream from Microsoft IP visible
RTP stream from internal client not consistently visible
10.07.25 10:50
Firewall logs reviewed.
New rule implemented:
Outbound traffic restricted
Only defined port ranges allowed
Documentation shows:
Teams media ports UDP 3478–3481
and dynamic RTP ports 49152–53247
Firewall rule allows only 3478–3481.
11.07.25 09:15

Temporary opening of wider UDP port range
→ Audio works immediately.
Port restricted again
→ No audio.
11.07.25 13:20
Comparison with internal architecture documentation:

“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1”
Section 3 describes:
• Media Bypass behavior
• RTP port range requirements
• Location-specific firewall rules
• NAT traversal
14.07.25

Zurich location uses Media Bypass
Other locations do not.
With Media Bypass, RTP runs directly from client to SBC.
Firewall rule was designed only for signaling.
16.07.25
Configuration adjustment carried out according to architecture specification.
Details not documented in the ticket for security reasons.
18.07.25 15:30
Multiple external test calls successful.
Audio stable in both directions.
Monitoring 48h without anomalies.

18.07.25 16:05
Dear Contact Person 14
The root cause was a restriction of media port communication following a firewall adjustment at the Zurich location.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.

Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Multiple types of printing failures (printer not responding after printers were moved into a new VLAN; single user “Access denied” from an explicit deny on the printer queue; default printer switching because of AD/GPO group membership; SAP printing broken after VM restore causing print‑share issues) were diagnosed and resolved by system engineers or required coordination with the Network/Core teams. Remediations included adjusting routing/firewall rules between server and printer VLAN, removing explicit deny permissions on the print queue, cleaning up AD group memberships or re‑enabling network shares on print servers — all actions done per the Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard or by engineering staff
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 22:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs
Ticket number: TI-1622483
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 15
Owner: Employee 21
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 06.11.25 08:37
Closure
Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52
Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored.
Activities
06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15
Good morning
Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed.
Other printers in the building are functioning normally.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
06.11.25 08:37
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21.
06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis
Remote access to an affected user.
Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing.
Queue itself is not paused.
Print Spooler service is running.
Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails.
06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location?
Thank you for your brief feedback.
06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response
Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN.
No other changes are known to us.
07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis
Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful.
Ping from a client within the same location network successful.
This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN.
Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly.
07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team
Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN.
Rule adjusted according to standard.
10.11.25 – Test phase
Printing from print server successful.
Multiple test prints from user workstations performed.
No abnormalities observed.
12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback
Printer is functioning normally again.
Larger print jobs are also processed correctly.
12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 15
The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company



SOURCE 3:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Single user cannot print – others in same office without issue
Ticket number: TI-1623488
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 20
Owner: Employee 27
Origin: Phone
Type: Incident
Created on: 02.12.25 09:03
Closure
Closure date: 03.12.25 10:21
Closure text: Permission on printer queue corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Printing possible again.
Activities
02.12.25 08:55 – Phone report
Mr. Contact Person 20 reports that one employee in the Basel office cannot print documents.
All other employees in the same room can print without issue.
The affected user receives the message “Access denied” when attempting to print.
02.12.25 09:03
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 27.
02.12.25 09:20 – Initial analysis
Remote connection established with the affected user.
Printer “MFP-Basel-01” is visible.
When sending a print job, “Access denied” appears immediately.
Print job is not displayed in the queue.
02.12.25 09:45 – Comparison with another user
Test performed with colleague in the same office.
Printing works without restriction.
User account checked.
Membership in location group “PRT-Basel” confirmed.
02.12.25 10:30 – Further review
Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
It specifies:
• Printer permissions are assigned exclusively via AD groups
• No individual user permissions at queue level
• NTFS and share permissions must be consistent
Review of the printer queue on the print server shows:
• AD group “PRT-Basel” has print permission
• Additionally, there is an explicit entry for the affected user with “Deny”
This entry originates from a previous test configuration.
02.12.25 11:10 – Test
Explicit deny permission removed.
User logged in again.
Test print successful.
03.12.25 09:40 – Customer feedback
Printing works normally again.
Additional tests within the team successful.
03.12.25 10:21 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 20
The root cause was an explicit “Deny” permission on the printer queue for the affected user.
The permission structure was cleaned up according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Printing is now fully functional again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company



SOURCE 2:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Incorrect printer automatically set as default
Ticket number: TI-1623017
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 18
Owner: Employee 23
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 18.11.25 08:49
Closure
Closure date: 24.11.25 15:11
Closure text: Printer assignment and default configuration corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Behavior stable.
Activities
18.11.25 08:35 – Email from Contact Person 18
Good morning
Several employees in the Finance department report that their default printer automatically switches to “MFP-Zuerich-01”, although they work at the Basel location.
They must manually select the correct printer before each print job.
The issue seems to have started last week.
Kind regards
18.11.25 08:49
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 23.
18.11.25 09:20 – Initial analysis
Remote connection established with an affected user.
Default printer is indeed “MFP-Zuerich-01”.
Both Basel and Zurich printers are visible in the printer list.
Comparison with a user from another department shows the correct location printer set.
18.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent organizational changes or group adjustments in the Finance department?
Kind regards
18.11.25 10:40 – Customer response
Yes, the Finance team was moved into a new AD group last week.
The old group was deactivated.
19.11.25 09:10 – GPO assignment analysis
Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Printer Provisioning it is defined that:
• Printer assignments are location-based via AD groups
• Default printers per location are set via GPO
• No parallel groups with identical function may exist
Review shows that the new Finance group is additionally a member of a higher-level Zurich location group.
As a result, the Zurich printer is also mapped via GPO and set as default.
19.11.25 11:30 – Test
Temporary removal of the incorrect group membership.
gpupdate /force executed.
User logged in again.
Default printer now correctly remains “MFP-Basel-01”.
20.11.25 – Coordination with customer
Confirmation that the group structure had not been fully cleaned up during the reorganization.
Correction performed according to blueprint specification.
24.11.25 14:50 – Customer feedback
Multiple users tested.
Default printer remains correct.
No further automatic switching.
24.11.25 15:11 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 18
The root cause was an inconsistent group membership following an organizational adjustment.
Printer assignment was corrected according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
The correct location printer now remains permanently set as default.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: The Zurich Teams symptom matches prior cases that were not solvable by client‑side steps (reinstalling, changing headsets, rebooting) and required SBC/network session and firewall changes performed by specialists (Direct Routing / NAT / session timer work)

Test call performed.
Audio immediately available in both directions.
Firewall reverted to restrictive rule.
Issue occurs again.
11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer
Good day
We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location.
Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked.
We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification.
We will provide an update shortly.
Kind regards
14.07.25 – Internal documentation review
Comparison with internal document
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1”
Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled.
Finding:
Zurich location configured with Media Bypass.
Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range.
16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment
Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
17.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We tested several external calls.
Audio works normally again.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you for your support.
Kind regards
18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure

The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment.

Consulting Sample Company

22.07.25 09:02 – Email from Contact Person 16 to Helpdesk
Good morning

22.07.25 09:14
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35.
22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day

– Are all locations affected or only a specific one?

22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response

22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note

External call to mobile phone.
Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds.
No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.”
23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis
Possible client error or unstable connection.
Test with another device → same behavior.
Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change.
23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer
Good day

23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response

24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis

24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis

There, 180 seconds.
25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation

25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests

UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: The Basel printing symptoms combine multiple failure modes seen before that required server/queue permission changes, AD/GPO corrections, or network/VLAN routing and firewall rule changes — each of which in the past involved system engineers and coordination with network/core teams, not just front‑line user fixes
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 22:

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs
Ticket number: TI-1622483
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 15
Owner: Employee 21
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 06.11.25 08:37
Closure
Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52
Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored.
Activities
06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15
Good morning
Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed.
Other printers in the building are functioning normally.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
06.11.25 08:37
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21.
06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis
Remote access to an affected user.
Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing.
Queue itself is not paused.
Print Spooler service is running.
Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails.
06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location?
Thank you for your brief feedback.
06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response
Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN.
No other changes are known to us.
07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis
Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful.
Ping from a client within the same location network successful.
This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN.
Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly.
07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team
Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN.
Rule adjusted according to standard.
10.11.25 – Test phase
Printing from print server successful.
Multiple test prints from user workstations performed.
No abnormalities observed.
12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback
Printer is functioning normally again.
Larger print jobs are also processed correctly.
12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 15
The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company



SOURCE 3:

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Single user cannot print – others in same office without issue
Ticket number: TI-1623488
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 20
Owner: Employee 27
Origin: Phone
Type: Incident
Created on: 02.12.25 09:03
Closure
Closure date: 03.12.25 10:21
Closure text: Permission on printer queue corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Printing possible again.
Activities
02.12.25 08:55 – Phone report
Mr. Contact Person 20 reports that one employee in the Basel office cannot print documents.
All other employees in the same room can print without issue.
The affected user receives the message “Access denied” when attempting to print.
02.12.25 09:03
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 27.
02.12.25 09:20 – Initial analysis
Remote connection established with the affected user.
Printer “MFP-Basel-01” is visible.
When sending a print job, “Access denied” appears immediately.
Print job is not displayed in the queue.
02.12.25 09:45 – Comparison with another user
Test performed with colleague in the same office.
Printing works without restriction.
User account checked.
Membership in location group “PRT-Basel” confirmed.
02.12.25 10:30 – Further review
Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
It specifies:
• Printer permissions are assigned exclusively via AD groups
• No individual user permissions at queue level
• NTFS and share permissions must be consistent
Review of the printer queue on the print server shows:
• AD group “PRT-Basel” has print permission
• Additionally, there is an explicit entry for the affected user with “Deny”
This entry originates from a previous test configuration.
02.12.25 11:10 – Test
Explicit deny permission removed.
User logged in again.
Test print successful.
03.12.25 09:40 – Customer feedback
Printing works normally again.
Additional tests within the team successful.
03.12.25 10:21 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 20
The root cause was an explicit “Deny” permission on the printer queue for the affected user.
The permission structure was cleaned up according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Printing is now fully functional again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Incorrect printer automatically set as default
Ticket number: TI-1623017
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 18
Owner: Employee 23
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 18.11.25 08:49
Closure
Closure date: 24.11.25 15:11
Closure text: Printer assignment and default configuration corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Behavior stable.
Activities
18.11.25 08:35 – Email from Contact Person 18
Good morning
Several employees in the Finance department report that their default printer automatically switches to “MFP-Zuerich-01”, although they work at the Basel location.
They must manually select the correct printer before each print job.
The issue seems to have started last week.
Kind regards
18.11.25 08:49
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 23.
18.11.25 09:20 – Initial analysis
Remote connection established with an affected user.
Default printer is indeed “MFP-Zuerich-01”.
Both Basel and Zurich printers are visible in the printer list.
Comparison with a user from another department shows the correct location printer set.
18.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent organizational changes or group adjustments in the Finance department?
Kind regards
18.11.25 10:40 – Customer response
Yes, the Finance team was moved into a new AD group last week.
The old group was deactivated.
19.11.25 09:10 – GPO assignment analysis
Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Printer Provisioning it is defined that:
• Printer assignments are location-based via AD groups
• Default printers per location are set via GPO
• No parallel groups with identical function may exist
Review shows that the new Finance group is additionally a member of a higher-level Zurich location group.
As a result, the Zurich printer is also mapped via GPO and set as default.
19.11.25 11:30 – Test
Temporary removal of the incorrect group membership.
gpupdate /force executed.
User logged in again.
Default printer now correctly remains “MFP-Basel-01”.
20.11.25 – Coordination with customer
Confirmation that the group structure had not been fully cleaned up during the reorganization.
Correction performed according to blueprint specification.
24.11.25 14:50 – Customer feedback
Multiple users tested.
Default printer remains correct.
No further automatic switching.
24.11.25 15:11 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 18
The root cause was an inconsistent group membership following an organizational adjustment.
Printer assignment was corrected according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
The correct location printer now remains permanently set as default.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Dear all,
I would like to inform you that we have identified the root cause and resolved the incident. Printing via both print servers through SAP is now functioning properly again. Mr. Contact Person 12 has confirmed that SAP print jobs are being executed correctly.
After the Windows update and the restore of the two VMs, all printers had issues with the network share on print server VRINFRGERPRNT03. After disabling and re-enabling the network share for all printer queues, all pending documents on the affected printers were successfully printed.
No further actions are currently required, and we will now close the ticket.
Should you require further assistance or have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.
Kind regards and have a nice weekend,
Employee 17
